sidebar.confirm_reset_body:
Yes: truncate all data and keep schema.
No: cancel.

dashboard_ui.no_data_body: Upload an IM or JO CSV file to generate your dashboard. The analysis will appear here automatically after finalization.
dashboard_ui.benchmarking_hint: Upload CSVs for other hotels in the same chain to enable cross-hotel benchmarking.

onboarding.page_subtitle: Import incident or job-order data. Select a file, choose an upload mode, and submit.
onboarding.dropzone_aria: Upload CSV file — drag and drop or click to browse

upload_mode.replace_desc: Overwrite existing records for this dataset scope.
upload_mode.append_desc: Add new records without removing previous records.
upload_mode.upsert_desc: Insert new records and update matching existing records.

kpi_notes_im.kpi_01: Total number of incidents logged in the selected period.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_02: Percentage of incidents resolved (Completed status) out of all incidents.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_03: Percentage of incidents still open or pending — not yet completed or cancelled.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_04: Count of incidents currently open or in a pending status.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_05: Count of incidents that were withdrawn or cancelled.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_06: Share of incidents tagged with VIP code.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_08: Share of incidents belonging to a repeated room + category + item combination.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_09: Average minutes between incident creation and first investigation update.
kpi_notes_im.kpi_10: Weighted average severity. Low=1, Medium=2, High=3, Critical=4.

kpi_notes_jo.kpi_01: Total volume of job orders in scope.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_04: Percentage of jobs ending in timeout.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_05: Share of jobs escalated for intervention.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_06: Share of jobs reassigned across teams.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_07: Average minutes from create to acknowledge.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_09: Average minutes from create to completion.
kpi_notes_jo.kpi_10: Total requested quantity across all jobs.

kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_01: Composite corporate health index balancing severity, VIP exposure, and SLA breach risk. Benchmark: Good >= 85, Watch 70-84.9, Bad < 70.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_02: Share of incidents classified as Critical. Benchmark: Good <= 1%, Watch 1-2%, Bad > 2%.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_03: Average cross-hotel benchmark index for fair chain-level comparison. Benchmark: Good >= 85, Watch 75-84.9, Bad < 75.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_04: Portion of incidents involving VIP guests; tracks premium-service risk. Benchmark: Good <= 6%, Watch 6-10%, Bad > 10%.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_05: Operational discipline KPI based on breach/late/overdue-like statuses. Benchmark: Good <= 3%, Watch 3-5%, Bad > 5%.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_06: Percentage of incidents that reached completed/closed state. Benchmark: Good >= 95%, Watch 90-94.9%, Bad < 90%.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_07: Resolution efficiency for VIP incidents. Benchmark: Good >= 95%, Watch 90-94.9%, Bad < 90%.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_08: Recurrence pressure indicator tied to loyalty/retention risk. Benchmark: Good <= 15%, Watch 15-25%, Bad > 25%.
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_09: Total number of incidents in the selected period. Benchmark: Good <= 800, Watch 801-1200, Bad > 1200 (thresholds should be tuned by property scale).
kpi_notes_im_corp.kpi_10: Concentration of incident volume in top 5 categories; higher can indicate systemic concentration risk. Benchmark: Good <= 45%, Watch 45-60%, Bad > 60%.

chart_titles_im.chart_14: Chain — VIP Incident Share by Hotel
chart_titles_im.chart_15: Chain — Avg Severity Score by Hotel
chart_titles_im.chart_20: Chain — Repeat Incident Rate by Hotel

chart_notes_im.eac_01: Click a status slice to drill down into its department breakdown.
chart_notes_im.eac_02: Count of incidents at each severity level. Critical and High require immediate management attention.
chart_notes_im.eac_03: Daily incident count over the period. Spikes indicate high-activity days.
chart_notes_im.eac_04: Most frequent incident categories. Focus improvement efforts on the leaders.
chart_notes_im.eac_05: Distribution of complaint sources and linked operating departments.
chart_notes_im.eac_06: Shows VIP incident concentration in the selected period.
chart_notes_im.chart_01: All incident categories ranked by volume.
chart_notes_im.chart_02: Proportional share of each severity level.
chart_notes_im.chart_03: Proportion of incidents in each status.
chart_notes_im.chart_04: Daily incident volume. Use to identify spikes and weekly rhythms.
chart_notes_im.chart_06: Incident distribution by day of the week.
chart_notes_im.chart_09: Status breakdown within the top 8 categories.
chart_notes_im.chart_10: Severity distribution for the top 5 incident categories.
chart_notes_im.chart_11: Percentage of incidents resolved within each category.
chart_notes_im.chart_12: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded. Upload additional hotels to enable cross-hotel comparison.
chart_notes_im.chart_13: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded.
chart_notes_im.chart_14: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded.
chart_notes_im.chart_15: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded.
chart_notes_im.chart_16: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded.
chart_notes_im.chart_17: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded.
chart_notes_im.chart_18: Incident density across departments and categories. Identifies which departments handle which categories most.
chart_notes_im.chart_19: Weekly incident count. Useful for identifying multi-week trends and seasonal patterns.
chart_notes_im.chart_20: Chain comparison not available — only one hotel uploaded.
chart_notes_im.chart_21: Incident distribution across 24 hours. Identifies peak operational hours for staffing decisions.
chart_notes_im.chart_28: Executive scorecard matrix across hotels for risk, critical, VIP, SLA, and trend metrics.
chart_notes_im.chart_29: Hotels ranked by weighted operational risk for intervention prioritization.
chart_notes_im.chart_30: Bubble quadrant: incident volume (x), average severity (y), VIP count (bubble size).
chart_notes_im.chart_31: Region-level heatmap to compare critical, VIP, SLA breach, and trend intensity.
chart_notes_im.chart_32: Corporate weekly incident trend with moving average smoothing.
chart_notes_im.chart_33: Heatmap of hotel versus department risk intensity.
chart_notes_im.chart_34: Pareto analysis of top root causes with cumulative percentage curve.
chart_notes_im.chart_35: Open critical aging proxy by hotel using current open+critical burden.
chart_notes_im.chart_36: Cross-hotel department case matrix for benchmarking.
chart_notes_im.chart_37: Hotel breach-rate leaderboard against SLA target line.

chart_titles_jo.jo_eac_01: Cumulative Weekly Service Category Share (Donut Race)
chart_titles_jo.jo_eac_03: Closing Rate vs Jobs by week
chart_titles_jo.jo_chart_02: JO Closing Rate vs Jobs Trend by week
chart_titles_jo.jo_chart_03: SLA Compliance vs Jobs Trend by week
chart_titles_jo.jo_chart_14: SLA Breach Minutes by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)
chart_titles_jo.jo_chart_15: Escalation by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)
chart_titles_jo.jo_chart_18: Resolution P90 by Service Category -> Service Items (Drilldown)

chart_notes_jo.jo_eac_01: Animated cumulative weekly donut race showing long-run share shifts by service category. Impact: sustained cumulative dominance reveals structural demand pressure points. Resolution: rebalance capacity plans, inventory, and preventive actions toward categories with persistent cumulative growth.
chart_notes_jo.jo_eac_02: Week-ascending workload bars with SLA compliance line.
chart_notes_jo.jo_eac_03: Week-ascending workload bars with closing rate line.
chart_notes_jo.jo_eac_04: Click a status slice to drill down into service category mix.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_02: Weekly workload (bars) versus closure efficiency (line) in ascending week order. Impact: rising jobs with falling close rate indicates backlog risk. Resolution: add short-term capacity, prioritize aging jobs, and enforce daily closure targets until rate stabilizes.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_03: Compares weekly incoming volume with SLA performance in ascending week order. Impact: SLA dips during high-volume weeks reveal process bottlenecks. Resolution: deploy surge playbook, tighten handoff SLAs, and monitor breach-prone queues hourly.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_04: Weekly timeout volume trend to detect service interruptions early. Impact: timeout spikes reduce guest satisfaction and increase repeat contacts. Resolution: identify root-cause weeks, fix routing/escalation delays, and set alert thresholds for timeout spikes.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_05: Vertical stacked view of status mix across the top 10 departments by volume. Impact: high open/pending share in specific departments signals queue congestion. Resolution: redistribute tickets, clear blockers, and set department-level WIP limits with daily review.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_06: Shows demand (bars) and close rate (line) by top categories. Impact: high-volume/low-close categories are critical performance gaps. Resolution: assign category owners, create playbooks, and track close-rate recovery by category weekly.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_07: Ranks the most requested service items. Impact: item concentration can create recurring operational strain. Resolution: bundle common tasks, automate repetitive steps, and pre-allocate resources for top items.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_08: Shows departments receiving the highest assignment load. Impact: uneven load can cause response delays and burnout. Resolution: rebalance dispatch rules and cross-train teams to absorb peaks.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_09: Shows request-origin departments generating the most JOs. Impact: large demand sources may indicate upstream process gaps. Resolution: run preventive actions with source departments to reduce avoidable requests.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_10: Shows departments completing the highest JO volume. Impact: low completion share versus assignment share may indicate execution bottlenecks. Resolution: compare assigned vs completed mix and remove completion blockers.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_11: Highlights locations with the largest JO demand. Impact: hotspots can degrade on-site service quality if unmanaged. Resolution: deploy location-specific staffing, stock, and preventive maintenance actions.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_12: Escalation concentration by department to identify process stress points.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_13: Reassignment concentration by department to identify routing inefficiency.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_14: Total breach minutes concentration by category with item drilldown. Impact: concentrated breach minutes identify where SLA risk is financially and reputationally highest. Resolution: prioritize chronic breach items for process redesign and escalation governance.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_15: Escalation concentration by category; click for item-level problem areas. Impact: high escalation indicates service instability or unclear ownership. Resolution: strengthen first-line decision rights, update runbooks, and clarify escalation triggers.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_16: Departments with the highest reassignment volume. Impact: frequent reassignment adds cycle time and accountability gaps. Resolution: improve assignment accuracy rules, skill mapping, and triage quality at intake.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_17: Resolution duration profile by department for identifying long-tail execution delays.
chart_notes_jo.jo_chart_18: P90 resolution time shows worst-case completion behavior by category and item. Impact: tail resolution delays drive complaints and SLA penalties. Resolution: target root-cause items with dedicated recovery plans and stricter completion SLAs.

imc_chart_map.imc02: Total Incident vs Status by Hotel
imc_chart_map.imc03: VIP Closure Rate vs VIP Incident by Hotel
imc_chart_map.imc05: Chain — Repeat Incident Rate by Hotel

imc_chart_notes.imc01: Shows each hotel total then top 10 incident items for drilldown prioritization. Benchmark: Good when top 3 items <= 45% of hotel incidents; Bad when top 3 items > 60% (concentration risk).
imc_chart_notes.imc02: Compares hotel volume and status mix to detect closure imbalance. Benchmark: Good when Completed >= 95% and Pending <= 5%; Bad when Pending > 10%.
imc_chart_notes.imc03: Dual-axis chart for premium guest risk and recovery effectiveness. Benchmark: Good VIP Closure >= 95%; Bad < 90%.
imc_chart_notes.imc04: Drilldown from hotel totals to top 10 categories for root-cause governance. Benchmark: Good when top category <= 20%; Bad when top category > 35%.
imc_chart_notes.imc05: Shows recurrence pressure by hotel to flag unresolved systemic issues. Benchmark: Good <= 15%; Watch 15–25%; Bad > 25%.
imc_chart_notes.imc06: Country-level map with hotel-level labels for cross-region executive visibility. Benchmark: Good when no single country exceeds 50% of chain incidents; Bad when one country > 70%.
imc_chart_notes.imc07: Hotel-to-department drilldown for operational ownership alignment. Benchmark: Good when no department exceeds 25% of hotel incidents; Bad > 40%.
imc_chart_notes.imc08: Hotel-to-source drilldown for channel quality control. Benchmark: Good when Unknown Source <= 5%; Bad > 15%.
imc_chart_notes.imc09: Stacked comparison of VIP and non-VIP load by hotel. Benchmark: Good VIP Share <= 6%; Watch 6–10%; Bad > 10%.
imc_chart_notes.imc10: Drilldown from hotel totals to booking source composition for commercial insights. Benchmark: Good when Unknown booking <= 5%; Bad > 15%.
imc_chart_notes.imc11: Executive matrix comparing risk, critical, VIP, SLA, and trend in one panel. Benchmark: Good risk score <= 60; Watch 60–100; Bad > 100.
imc_chart_notes.imc12: Ranks hotels by weighted risk for intervention priority. Benchmark: Good when high-risk hotel count reduces period-over-period; Bad when top hotel risk grows >10% WoW.
imc_chart_notes.imc13: Bubble quadrant for strategic risk classification (high volume + high severity = immediate focus). Benchmark: Good when no hotel in top-right high-risk quadrant; Bad when multiple hotels cluster there.
imc_chart_notes.imc14: Region matrix compares critical, VIP, SLA breach and trend intensity. Benchmark: Good when all risk cells trend down or stay green; Bad when >=2 metrics red in same region.
imc_chart_notes.imc15: Shows department risk intensity by hotel to target governance actions. Benchmark: Good when top department risk <= 20% of hotel total; Bad > 35%.
imc_chart_notes.imc16: Ranks root causes and cumulative contribution for improvement prioritization. Benchmark: Good when top 5 causes <= 45%; Bad when top 5 > 60%.
imc_chart_notes.imc17: Highlights unresolved critical burden (aging proxy) by hotel for escalation governance. Benchmark: Good = 0 open critical aging; Bad when persistent > 3 cases.
imc_chart_notes.imc18: Cross-hotel department matrix for fast benchmarking and imbalance detection. Benchmark: Good when cross-hotel variance is balanced; Bad when one department dominates across multiple hotels.

imc_chart_formulas.imc01: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_item_name per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.imc03: VIP Incidents = COUNT(vip_code valid) GROUP BY hotel; VIP Closure % = VIP Completed / VIP Incidents * 100
imc_chart_formulas.imc04: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = TOP 10 COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY incident_category per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.imc05: Repeat Rate % = repeat_count / total_cases * 100 per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.imc06: Country Value = SUM(total_cases) GROUP BY country_code; Label = CONCAT(hotel_code, incident_count) list per country
imc_chart_formulas.imc07: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY department per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.imc08: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY source_of_complaint per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.imc09: VIP = COUNT(vip_code valid); Non-VIP = total_cases - VIP; GROUP BY hotel_code
imc_chart_formulas.imc10: Level 1 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code; Level 2 = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY booking_source per hotel
imc_chart_formulas.imc13: X=COUNT(cases), Y=AVG(severity score), Bubble=VIP cases, Color=country/region
imc_chart_formulas.imc14: Regional KPI = AVG(metric by hotel in region); Regional Risk = aggregate of weighted KPI intensities
imc_chart_formulas.imc15: Department Risk Proxy = COUNT(cases) by hotel_code + department (or weighted severity where available)
imc_chart_formulas.imc17: Open Critical Aging Proxy = MIN(critical_cases, pending_cases) by hotel (aging date fallback when explicit age not present)
imc_chart_formulas.imc18: Matrix Cell = COUNT(incident_case) GROUP BY hotel_code, department
